title: QUIC CRT server client-auth transcript/runtime prerequisite work item
completion_notes: This work item lands the public `ClientCertificateRequired` gate on the existing `SslServerAuthenticationOptions` carrier after the internal transcript/runtime prerequisite is in place. Callback-driven acceptance is supported, while broader chain/revocation customization, broader PKI, 0-RTT, key update, transfer, retry, and client-side certificate-presentation remain out of scope.
out_of_scope: broader client-auth policy beyond the requested server-auth slice
summary: Open the existing public server carrier to callback-driven `ClientCertificateRequired` acceptance on top of the already-landed managed server-auth prerequisite, without widening broader PKI, transfer, or retry behavior.
verification_plan: Run focused requirement-home tests for the public validator gate and the internal server-auth path, re-run the current CRT and API guards that must remain unchanged, re-run the full REQ_QUIC_CRT_ sweep, and run repo-local SpecTrace validation/render checks after the canonical JSON updates.
planned_changes:
- Open the existing public server carrier to `ClientCertificateRequired` only where the callback-driven acceptance seam exists.
- Emit one TLS 1.3 `CertificateRequest` on the server flight when the server-auth floor is enabled.
- Accept an inbound client `Certificate` / `CertificateVerify` / `Finished` sequence in the server-role transcript and key schedule.
- Surface client-certificate acceptance only through the existing callback seam and fail closed if acceptance is not granted.
- Keep explicit chain/revocation customization deferred on this slice.
- Add positive and negative requirement-home tests that prove the public gate, the internal prerequisite slice, and the rejection paths without claiming broader PKI, 0-RTT, transfer, retry, or key-update behavior.
